days.note: Day trip from Kyoto — morning Nara, afternoon Uji

activities.name: Train: Kyoto → Nara
activities.notes: JR Nara Line from Kyoto Station to Nara Station — ~45 min, ¥720. Depart early to beat tour groups at Todai-ji. Kintetsu Limited Express is faster (35 min, ¥1,130) but JR pass covers the regular train.

activities.name: Kofuku-ji & Sarusawa Pond
activities.notes: First stop after the station — the five-story pagoda is immediately visible. Sarusawa Pond reflects the pagoda perfectly. Kofuku-ji is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, free to walk the grounds.

activities.name: Nara Park — Deer & Todai-ji
activities.notes: Over 1,000 wild sika deer roam freely — they bow for deer crackers (shika senbei, ¥200/pack sold by vendors). Walk through the park to Todai-ji: the world's largest wooden building, housing a 15m bronze Great Buddha. ¥600 entry. Arriving before 10am means you beat the tour groups.

activities.name: 🍴 Lunch — Kakinoha-zushi
activities.notes: Nara's signature dish: small pressed sushi wrapped in persimmon leaves, which act as a natural preservative and add a subtle fragrance. Try Hiraso (near Kintetsu Nara Station) — one of the most respected kakinoha-zushi shops, established 1861.
activities.place: Kintetsu Nara Station area, Nara, Japan

activities.name: Kasuga-taisha Shrine
activities.notes: One of Japan's most celebrated Shinto shrines, deep in a primeval forest. Famous for thousands of bronze and stone lanterns lining the approach. UNESCO World Heritage. ¥500 entry to inner precinct.

activities.name: Train: Nara → Uji
activities.notes: JR Nara Line from Nara Station to Uji Station — ~28 min, ¥420. Direct — no transfer needed. Uji is on the same line between Nara and Kyoto.
